page: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Fasv6 family. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, operating at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is provisioned with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and runs under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local ephemeral storage but supports Premium Storage options.

Benchmark data indicates exceptional single-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including software compilation with Clang and ray tracing, falls into the average category. A notable hardware trade-off is the memory subsystem; while small-block cached reads and large-block writes are average, large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%.

This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution speed and dedicated CPU allocation over massive memory bandwidth. It is highly suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis databases, and lightweight text processing. However, it is less optimal for memory-bound applications or large-scale multi-threaded tasks due to the uncached read bandwidth limitations.
description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Fasv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 16.0 GB of RAM and lacks local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench scores. However, multi-core operations and uncached large-block memory reads perform in the average and weak tiers, respectively. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high single-thread performance and dedicated CPU resources, such as lightweight web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting, while presenting trade-offs for memory-bandwidth-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suitable for web serving and general workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4as_v6 is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering top-tier single-core performance and balanced memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with no local ephemeral storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in the top 10%
Average multi-core and memory write bandwidth capabilities
Best suited for general-purpose hosting, web serving, and single-threaded tasks
